turns.content: someone told me i should move to dallas for real estate. like the market is better there or whatever
Dallas does have a pretty hot real estate market right now. Are you seriously considering it, or just thinking out loud?
nah bro im not going anywhere. houston is home
Sounds like your mind is pretty set. What keeps you locked in to Houston?
everything. my whole network is here. the people i know the community. you dont just rebuild that somewhere else
That makes total sense. Years of relationships and connections — that's not something you can just replicate in a new city. Especially in real estate, knowing people is half the game.
exactly bro. and ismail is here. amira is here. im not about to be a weekend dad on facetime thats not how i do it
Being physically present for your son — that's non-negotiable for you. FaceTime parenting wouldn't cut it, and honestly, it shouldn't have to.
wallahi. and i know houston. i know the neighborhoods the areas where people are buying. thats my advantage for real estate
Three years of driving around every corner of the city is basically free market research. You probably know neighborhoods better than agents who've been licensed for years.
bro thats what im saying. i know which areas are coming up which ones are falling off. i see it every day from the car
That's a legit edge. You're watching the city change in real time — new construction, which blocks are getting busier, where people are moving to. That's hard data you can't get from a website.
facts. so yeah houston is the move. not leaving. just gotta level up right here where i am
Level up in place — I respect that. You've got the roots, the knowledge, and the motivation all right here. No reason to start over somewhere else when you can build on what you already have.
inshallah bro. this time next year im licensed and closing deals
Inshallah. With how locked in you are, I wouldn't bet against you. This time next year is going to look different.
